authoritative_sources.title: 14 CFR Part 21 - Certification Procedures for Products and Articles
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This is the primary FAA regulatory baseline for production approvals, conformity, and release-boundary expectations in aerospace manufacturing.

authoritative_sources.title: Advisory Circular 21-43A, Production Under 14 CFR Part 21, Subparts F, G, K, and O
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA AC 21-43A is official production guidance defining quality-system and control obligations for approved production approval holders.

authoritative_sources.title: Certificate Management / Quality System Audit (QSA) of Production Approval Holders
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FAA QSA guidance describes how production-approval quality systems are evaluated and therefore is directly relevant to escalation and audit-readiness handling.

authoritative_sources.title: 9100 Quality Management Systems - Requirements for Aviation, Space and Defense Organizations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG 9100 defines the recognized aerospace quality-management framework and evidence vocabulary used in this lane.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality-system structure and assurance workflow review

authoritative_sources.title: Supply Chain Management Handbook
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAQG SCMH is an implementation reference for supplier-quality, traceability, and supplier-control practices in aerospace programs.
used_for: supplier qualification and substitution decision boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: NASA-STD-8739.6B, Implementation Requirements for NASA Workmanship Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: This public workmanship standard is directly relevant to aerospace workmanship control, inspection evidence, and documentation quality expectations.

workflow_stages.name: quality-system evidence review
workflow_stages.description: Review production, inspection, nonconformance, supplier, and release records to determine whether evidence supports bounded recommendations.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
Public manufacturing, quality, or safety standards

workflow_stages.name: hold, release, and nonconformance boundary handling
workflow_stages.description: Preserve hold and release boundaries, identify unresolved evidence gaps, and route formal authority to engineering, supplier, or quality release owners.
source_classes: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: supplier and traceability escalation
workflow_stages.description: Evaluate supplier records, certificates, lot and serial genealogy, and receiving controls before routing substitution or release posture decisions.
source_classes: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: defect, incident, and audit-readiness handling
workflow_stages.description: Organize repeat defects, recurring nonconformances, CAPA status, and audit-readiness findings into bounded escalation packages.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

artifacts: MES execution records, as-built and work-in-process history
inspection reports, first-article evidence, and acceptance records
MRB, nonconformance, CAPA, and incident records
supplier certifications, COAs, source inspection artifacts, and corrective-action responses
lot, serial, and batch traceability records
quality release records and authority logs

allowed: assess whether visible aerospace quality evidence supports hold, release recommendation, CAPA escalation, or audit-readiness findings
organize missing records and open dependencies with owner routing
summarize lot or serial traceability conflicts and supplier-quality issues
escalate: final engineering disposition, DER/ODA approval, FAA release, or regulator-facing communication
supplier approval, substitution, or commercial release authority decisions
formal legal, export-control, safety-critical, or contractual interpretation that changes disposition
refuse: override documented hold, release, traceability, or supplier controls
conclude final airworthiness compliance from incomplete records
approve substitution or closure where required external approvals are unresolved

domain_failure_modes: traceability records conflict across ERP, MES, WMS, and supplier evidence
product is pressured toward release while holds, MRB, or inspection evidence is incomplete
repeat defects are treated as closed without corrective-action evidence
supplier substitutions or COA exceptions are accepted without documented authority
quality review shifts into final engineering or regulatory decision-making

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Safety or quality evidence incomplete or contradictory

unresolved_ambiguities.note: The specialist preserves tenant-supplied criticality and mission classification as a prerequisite because public sources do not fully determine whether a defect route requires design authority escalation for every item.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Release authority for OEM program-defined waivers
unresolved_ambiguities.note: The pack requires explicit tenant workflow maps for delegated approvals and waiver routes before any release or closure recommendation is issued.

notes: The public baseline supports aerospace quality evidence and control handling, but tenant retrieval is still required for program flowdowns, release authority maps, and current system-of-record locations.
No blocking ambiguities remain because unresolved jurisdiction or authority questions trigger an escalation/return posture and block confident conclusions.
